title: [ci-scan-net11] Issue33287.DisplayAlertAsyncShouldNotCrashWhenPageUnloaded consistently times out on MacCatalyst (net11.0)
author: github-actions[bot]
body:
## Summary
`Issue33287.DisplayAlertAsyncShouldNotCrashWhenPageUnloaded` consistently fails on MacCatalyst in `maui-pr-uitests` with `System.TimeoutException: Timed out waiting for element`. The failure persists through retries (2 of 2 attempts fail) indicating this is not a transient flake.

## Build Information
- **Pipeline**: maui-pr-uitests
- **Build**: [1498914](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1498914)
- **Build ID**: 1498914
- **Branch**: net11.0
- **First seen**: 2026-07-07 (build 1496867)
- **Occurrences**: 2 in last 10 builds (builds 1496867 and 1498914), both with persistent retry failure

## Affected Legs
- `Controls (MacCatalyst)` — `catalyst.cake` — MacCatalyst 15.3 — TestCategory=Page|Picker
- `Controls (MacCatalyst)` — `catalyst.cake` — MacCatalyst 15.3 (retry run)

## Error Message
```
Failed DisplayAlertAsyncShouldNotCrashWhenPageUnloaded [33 s]
  Error Message:
   System.TimeoutException : Timed out waiting for element...
  Stack Trace:
     at UITest.Appium.HelperExtensions.WaitForElement(...)
     at Microsoft.Maui.TestCases.Tests.Issues.Issue33287.DisplayAlertAsyncShouldNotCrashWhenPageUnloaded()
        in src/Controls/tests/TestCases.Shared.Tests/Tests/Issues/Issue33287.cs:line 28
```

## Recommended Action
1. Inspect `src/Controls/tests/TestCases.Shared.Tests/Tests/Issues/Issue33287.cs` — the test calls `DisplayAlertAsync` then unloads the page and waits for a UI element that never appears.
2. Check whether the `DisplayAlert` dialog is being presented asynchronously and the page unload races with the alert dismiss on MacCatalyst.
3. Run locally on MacCatalyst to confirm the element that is waited on (`WaitForElement`) is never rendered or is rendered under a different accessibility id.
4. If this is a test design issue (timing too tight on Mac), consider adding a platform-specific timeout or a `Task.Delay` guard. If it is a real regression in `DisplayAlertAsync`, add a fix to `src/Controls/src/Core/Page.cs` or the MacCatalyst handler.

title: [ci-scan-net11] Recurring UI test failures — Image/ImageButton visual-baseline mismatch and URI image load error on iOS (net11.0)
author: github-actions[bot]
body:
## Summary
Multiple `Image` and `ImageButton` UI tests fail on iOS in `maui-pr-uitests`: visual-baseline snapshot comparisons diverge by ~27% for URI-sourced images, and `LoadTestImageButtonShouldLoadImageWithoutException` reports "Status: Error occurred" instead of a successful load.

## Build Information
- **Pipeline**: maui-pr-uitests
- **Build**: [1501538](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1501538)
- **Build ID**: 1501538
- **Branch**: net11.0
- **First seen**: 2026-07-08 (build 1499382)
- **Occurrences**: 3 in last 10 builds (1501538, 1501107, 1499382)

## Affected Legs
- `Controls (v18.5) Image,ImageButton,IndicatorView,...` — iOS 18.5 simulator
- `Controls (vlatest) Image,ImageButton,...` — iOS latest simulator
- `Controls Image,ImageButton,...` — Android API 30

## Error Message
```
Failed VerifyImageAspect_AspectFillWithImageSourceFromUri [6 s]
  VisualTestUtils.VisualTestFailedException :
  Snapshot different than baseline: VerifyImageAspect_AspectFillWithImageSourceFromUri.png (27.12% difference)
  If the correct baseline has changed (this isn't a bug), then update the baseline image.
  See test attachment or download the build artifacts to get the new snapshot file.

Failed VerifyImageButtonAspect_AspectFillWithImageSourceFromUri [6 s]
  (same 27% visual diff for AspectFit, Center, Fill variants)

Failed LoadTestImageButtonShouldLoadImageWithoutException [1 s]
  Expected: "Status: Image loaded successfully"
  But was:  "Status: Error occurred"

Failed DownSizeImageAppearProperly [2 s]
  (visual baseline mismatch)
```

## Recommended Action
1. **`VerifyImageAspect/VerifyImageButtonAspect` (URI variants)**: The 27% visual diff suggests images loaded from a URI are not rendering at all (blank/placeholder) or rendering differently. Check if the test URI (`(redacted)`) is reachable from Helix machines. If the images can't load from the internet, they render as empty — causing the diff.
2. **`LoadTestImageButtonShouldLoadImageWithoutException`**: "Status: Error occurred" confirms a runtime error loading an image from a URI. Likely the same network-access issue.
3. **`DownSizeImageAppearProperly`**: If this test also uses a URI image source, same root cause.
4. **Fix options**: (a) Replace external URI image sources with local embedded resources in the test app so CI machines don't need internet access. (b) Add `[Skip]` with network condition. (c) Update visual baselines if the rendering intentionally changed.
5. Check `src/Controls/tests/TestCases.Shared.Tests/Tests/` for the `ImageTests` and `ImageButtonTests` test classes.

title: [windows, iOS & macOS]Grouped CollectionView with GridItemsLayout renders inconsistent layout across platforms
author: LogishaSelvarajSF4525
body:
### Description

When using a CollectionView with GridItemsLayout (Span > 1) and grouping enabled, the layout is rendered inconsistently across platforms.

The CollectionView is configured with:
GridItemsLayout
Span = 5
Orientation = Vertical
VerticalItemSpacing set to a non-zero value (for example, 10)
IsGrouped = true

### Steps to Reproduce

Clone the .NET MAUI repository and open it in Visual Studio.
Build and deploy the TestCases.HostApp project.
Navigate to Issue35700 in the HostApp(https://github.com/dotnet/maui/blob/inflight/candidate/src/Controls/tests/TestCases.HostApp/Issues/Issue35700.cs)
Run the sample on Windows, iOS, or macOS.
Observe the layout of the grouped CollectionView.

**Actual behavior**
The layout differs across platforms:
- Windows: The first item in the first group is rendered smaller than the other items.
- iOS: No spacing is applied between the group header and the first row of items.
- macOS: No spacing is applied between the group header and the first row of items.

**Expected behavior**
The grouped CollectionView should render consistently across all platforms:
- All items should have a uniform size.
- The configured VerticalItemSpacing should be applied consistently.
- The spacing between the group header and the first row of items should match the expected layout on every platform.

✅ **Just merged:** [#36478](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/pull/36478) — `SwipeItemView.Command` now uses `WeakCommandSubscription` (fixes #36343).

**Outstanding leak issues awaiting fixes:** `ListView.RefreshCommand` (#36344), `BackButtonBehavior.Command` (#36354), `IndicatorView.ItemsSource` (#36352, #36347).

  Completes the iOS 26 large-title fix for non-Shell `NavigationPage` scenarios. Covers `ScrollView`, `Grid`, `ListView`, and `CollectionView` paths with regression coverage.

title: [ci-scan] Recurring: PickerDialogDoesNotCrashWhenPagePoppedWhileDialogOpen (Issue31731) flaky on macOS in maui-pr-uitests
author: github-actions[bot]
body:
## Summary
`Issue31731.PickerDialogDoesNotCrashWhenPagePoppedWhileDialogOpen` intermittently fails on macOS with `System.TimeoutException: Timed out waiting for element` in the `Controls Page,Performance,Picker,ProgressBar` UI test leg of `maui-pr-uitests`.

## Build Information
- **Pipeline**: maui-pr-uitests (definition 313)
- **Build**: [1503332](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1503332) / [1486368](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1486368)
- **Build ID**: 1503332
- **Branch**: main
- **First seen**: 2026-06-29 (build 1486368)
- **Occurrences**: 2 in last 10 builds (1486368 on 2026-06-29, 1503332 on 2026-07-10)

## Affected Legs
- macOS UITests — `Controls Page,Performance,Picker,ProgressBar` task (macOS Catalyst stage)
- Test file: `src/Controls/tests/TestCases.Shared.Tests/Tests/Issues/Issue31731.cs`

## Error Message
```
Failed PickerDialogDoesNotCrashWhenPagePoppedWhileDialogOpen [29 s]
  Error Message:
   System.TimeoutException : Timed out waiting for element...
  Stack Trace:
     at UITest.Appium.HelperExtensions.Wait(...) in HelperExtensions.cs:line 2757
     at UITest.Appium.HelperExtensions.WaitForElement(...) in HelperExtensions.cs:line 795
     at Microsoft.Maui.TestCases.Tests.Issues.Issue31731.PickerDialogDoesNotCrashWhenPagePoppedWhileDialogOpen()
       in Issue31731.cs:line 40
```

In some retry attempts (1486368) the second run also failed with `StaleElementReferenceException` on the `colorPicker` TextField, indicating a race condition between the Picker dialog dismissal and page pop.

## Recommended Action
1. Open `src/Controls/tests/TestCases.Shared.Tests/Tests/Issues/Issue31731.cs` — the test opens a `Picker` dialog and pops the page while the dialog is open, verifying no crash occurs.
2. The `WaitForElement` at line 40 times out, suggesting the expected element is not visible after the page pop + dialog interaction sequence on macOS.
3. On macOS Catalyst, `Picker` uses a popover (`UIPopoverController`) which may not be dismissed synchronously when the page is popped. Consider adding an explicit wait for the popover to dismiss before asserting the final state.
4. The `StaleElementReferenceException` on `colorPicker` TextField in the retry indicates the element was removed from the view hierarchy mid-query — typical of a popover whose content view was deallocated. This is likely a macOS-specific timing issue.
5. Investigate recent changes to `src/Controls/src/Core/Handlers/Picker/PickerHandler.MaciOS.cs` or `src/Controls/src/Core/Platform/MaciOS/` for any changes to popover lifecycle.

body:
## Summary
`Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.UnitTests` fails on Windows Helix with `System.IO.IOException: The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process` in `StreamingJsonDeserializerTests+FileBasedTests`. Tests appear to share access to test data files without proper isolation.

## Build Information
- **Pipeline**: maui-pr
- **Build**: [1498916](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1498916)
- **Build ID**: 1498916
- **Branch**: net11.0
- **First seen**: 2026-07-07 (build 1497603)
- **Occurrences**: 2 in last 10 builds

## Affected Legs
- `Windows Helix Unit Tests (Debug)` — `Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.UnitTests` / Windows.10.Amd64.Open
- `Windows Helix Unit Tests (Release)` — same test assembly (suspected)

## Error Message
```
Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.UnitTests.StreamingJsonDeserializerTests+FileBasedTests.ProcessChunk_TxtFile_FinalResultMatchesDirectDeserialization [FAIL]
  System.IO.IOException : The process cannot access the file
  'C:\h\w\...\TestData\DataStreams\Itinerary\sahara-itinerary-1.txt'
  because it is being used by another process.
  Stack Trace:
    at Microsoft.Win32.SafeHandles.SafeFileHandle.CreateFile(...)
    at System.IO.File.ReadAllLines(String path, Encoding encoding)
    /_/src/AI/tests/Essentials.AI.UnitTests/Tests/StreamingJsonDeserializerTests/FileBasedTests.cs(44,0)

Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.UnitTests  Total: 333, Errors: 0, Failed: 1, Skipped: 0
```

Also seen (build 1497603 / 1498916):
```
ProcessChunk_TxtFile_ShouldProgressDaysArray [FAIL]
  System.IO.IOException: The process cannot access the file
  'C:\h\...\TestData\DataStreams\Itinerary\maui-itinerary-2.txt'
  because it is being used by another process.
```

## Recommended Action
1. **Investigate `FileBasedTests.cs`** in `src/AI/tests/Essentials.AI.UnitTests/Tests/StreamingJsonDeserializerTests/` — check whether multiple test cases open the same files concurrently without `FileShare.ReadWrite` or proper locking.
2. If tests run in parallel (xUnit default on this assembly), add `[Collection("FileBasedTests")]` or use a `CollectionDefinition` to serialize file-sharing tests.
3. Alternatively, copy test data files to a per-test unique temp path (using `Path.GetTempFileName()` + copy) so parallel runs don't contend.
4. Confirm on the Helix console log: the failing work item is `Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.UnitTests.dll` in jobs `243a1cf3-f266-4b8c-b17f-184cba515eb3` (build 1497603) and `5ab9be21-3ab3-41bc-a000-8c01d9215be1` (build 1498916).
